stereotype: «ResourceAccess» — owns operated_system_state (Postgres CNPG head-state aggregate: one row per operated app + applied_mutation dedup ledger)
volatility: OperatedSystem head-state aggregate — current state of an operated application: published desired state, runtime status, in-flight flag, and delinquency posture

outbound.name: operated_system_state (Postgres)
outbound.how: direct, by value — plain Go methods; Activity-wrapped by operationsManager; SELECT…FOR UPDATE + guarded UPDATE + applied_mutation dedup ledger

ops.signature: publishDesiredState(operatedAppId: OperatedAppID, expectedVersion: Version, desired: DesiredState, reason: DesiredStateReason, decision: *AutoscaleDecision, idempotencyKey: fwra.IdempotencyKey) → Version
ops.note: Records desired state the platform published to runtime infrastructure; reason ∈ {operator, deployAfterConstruction, delinquency, autoscale}; decision carried for autoscale only; bounded recent-history maintained for the autoscaler

fields.name: operatedAppId
fields.note: string; identity of the operated app aggregate

fields.name: expectedVersion
fields.note: uint64; optimistic concurrency guard; Conflict returned if stale

fields.name: desired
fields.note: the desired state published to the runtime infrastructure (infrastructure-opaque struct: replicas, bundle ref, resource envelope)

fields.name: reason
fields.note: int enum: operator | deployAfterConstruction | delinquency | autoscale

fields.name: decision
fields.note: pointer; non-nil only when reason == autoscale; carries the autoscalerEngine decision that drove the publish

fields.name: idempotencyKey
fields.note: dedup token checked against applied_mutation ledger before head-state write (dedup-first ordering)

fields.name: version
fields.note: uint64; new head-state version after the write; caller uses for next expectedVersion

fields.name: (error)
fields.note: Method layer-typed error; Conflict = optimistic concurrency mismatch (Manager re-reads and re-applies with same idempotencyKey)

ops.signature: recordRuntimeStatusChange(operatedAppId: OperatedAppID, expectedVersion: Version, status: RuntimeStatus, idempotencyKey: fwra.IdempotencyKey) → Version
ops.note: Records an observed runtime-status transition; called only on transition per the reconcile tick

fields.name: operatedAppId
fields.note: string

fields.name: expectedVersion
fields.note: uint64; optimistic concurrency guard

fields.name: status
fields.note: int enum: Pending | Healthy | Degraded | Withdrawn (observed runtime phase)

fields.name: idempotencyKey
fields.note: dedup token; same key on replay returns without second write

fields.name: version
fields.note: uint64; new version after the write

fields.name: (error)
fields.note: Method layer-typed error

ops.signature: withdrawSystem(operatedAppId: OperatedAppID, expectedVersion: Version, idempotencyKey: fwra.IdempotencyKey) → Version
ops.note: Marks the operated system withdrawn (operator action or non-payment terminal)

fields.name: operatedAppId
fields.note: string

fields.name: expectedVersion
fields.note: uint64; optimistic concurrency guard

fields.name: idempotencyKey
fields.note: dedup token

fields.name: version
fields.note: uint64; new version after the write

fields.name: (error)
fields.note: Method layer-typed error

ops.signature: recordDelinquencyAction(operatedAppId: OperatedAppID, expectedVersion: Version, action: DelinquencyAction, idempotencyKey: fwra.IdempotencyKey) → Version
ops.note: Records a delinquency-handling action taken against the operated app

fields.name: operatedAppId
fields.note: string

fields.name: expectedVersion
fields.note: uint64; optimistic concurrency guard

fields.name: action
fields.note: int; the delinquency-handling action taken (e.g. pause, withdraw, escalate)

fields.name: idempotencyKey
fields.note: dedup token

fields.name: version
fields.note: uint64; new version after the write

fields.name: (error)
fields.note: Method layer-typed error

ops.note: Returns the whole head-state (desired state, bundle ref, runtime status, recent desired-state history, delinquency); NotFound if no row

fields.name: operatedAppId
fields.note: string; the operated app to read

fields.name: ID
fields.note: string

fields.name: Version
fields.note: uint64; current head-state version

fields.name: Desired
fields.note: the last desired state published to the runtime infrastructure

fields.name: Status
fields.note: int; observed runtime phase

fields.name: InFlight
fields.note: whether this app is currently in-flight (active)

fields.name: RecentDesired
fields.note: bounded recent desired-state history for the autoscaler (JSONB list, trimmed on write)

fields.name: Delinquency
fields.note: pointer; non-nil when a delinquency action has been taken

fields.name: (error)
fields.note: Method layer-typed error; NotFound if no row for operatedAppId

ops.note: Returns in-flight operated apps for a project/customer scope; drives the reconcile tick and the delinquency sweep; plain cross-row SELECT

fields.name: projectId
fields.note: project or customer scope to list in-flight apps for; maps to a status/in-flight predicate cross-row SELECT

fields.name: (slice element)
fields.note: struct{ ID OperatedAppID; Status RuntimeStatus; … }; lightweight summary for the reconcile tick and delinquency sweep

fields.name: (error)
fields.note: Method layer-typed error

dataContracts: DesiredStateReason = int // operator | deployAfterConstruction | delinquency | autoscale
RuntimeStatus = int // e.g. Pending | Healthy | Degraded | Withdrawn
DesiredState = struct{ /* replicas, bundle ref, resource envelope — infrastructure-opaque */ }
AutoscaleDecision = struct{ /* autoscalerEngine decision that drove autoscale publish */ }
OperatedSystem = struct{ ID OperatedAppID; Version Version; Desired DesiredState; Status RuntimeStatus; InFlight bool; RecentDesired []DesiredStatePublication; Delinquency *DelinquencyAction }
OperatedSystemSummary = struct{ ID OperatedAppID; Status RuntimeStatus; /* … */ }
errorModel: framework-go/resourceaccess.Error — Conflict (optimistic concurrency version mismatch; Manager re-reads and re-applies with same idempotency key) / NotFound / Transient / Infrastructure / ContractMisuse. Identical model to projectStateAccess. Write-verb discipline: dedup-first (applied_mutation ledger check before head-state write) + version guard (SELECT…FOR UPDATE + guarded UPDATE).
idempotency: Write verbs: version guard (expectedVersion → Conflict on stale) + dedup ledger (idempotencyKey → applied_mutation; dedup-first ordering preserves idempotent replay). Read verbs: pure reads, no key. RecentDesired bounded JSONB list trimmed on write.

revisions.byActivity: design-only (activity TBD when operationsManager is built)
revisions.summary: Initial design-only contract: 6 ops (4 write verbs + 2 reads); split from the original monolithic projectStateAccess; head-state aggregate keyed by operatedAppId; identical write discipline to projectStateAccess (version guard + dedup ledger); not implemented — operationsManager not yet built
